Summary: Cassandra, a princess of Troy, recounts her life story, from being the favored daughter of King Priam to becoming a cursed prophetess after Apollo granted her the gift of prophecy in exchange for her devotion. However, when she pulled away from his advances at 12 years old, he became displeased and cursed her to see the future but never be believed. Cassandra foresaw and tried to warn her family about the impending doom of Troy, including the deaths of her loved ones: Hector's body dragged by Achilles' chariot; Polydorus' body washing up on the shore; Polyxena being killed as a sacrifice; her father King Priam being killed in the same manner as his grandson Polydorus; and her own rape at the hands of Ajax the Lesser. After Troy falls, Cassandra is taken captive and awaits her fate along with other women of the city, including her mother who will be given to Odysseus, Andromache who will be given to Neoptolemus but end up marrying Helenus, Polyxena who died before being given as plunder, and Helen who will return to her husband. Cassandra herself will be given to Agamemnon, who will rape her, and she will bear him twins before he is murdered by his wife Clytemnestra in revenge for the murder of her daughter Iphigenia; Clytemnestra will then kill Cassandra with the same sword.

Cassandra foresaw and tried to warn her family about the impending doom of Troy, including the deaths of her loved ones: Hector's body dragged by Achilles' chariot; Polydorus' body washing up on the shore; Polyxena being killed as a sacrifice; her father King Priam being killed in the same manner as his grandson Polydorus; and her own rape at the hands of Ajax the Lesser. After Troy falls, Cassandra is taken captive and awaits her fate along with other women of the city, including her mother who will be given to Odysseus, Andromache who will be given to Neoptolemus but end up marrying Helenus, Polyxena who died before being given as plunder, and Helen who will return to her husband. Cassandra herself will be given to Agamemnon, who will rape her, and she will bear him twins before he is murdered by his wife Clytemnestra in revenge for the murder of her daughter Iphigenia; Clytemnestra will then kill Cassandra with the same sword.
Reason: Errors in: Mode of Demise (FP or FN) with at least one TP
